claims.claim: We investigate whether preserving and exploiting citation locator metadata within chunk text can improve evidence retrieval for citation-resolution tasks.

evidence_refs.quote: # Citation Locator Metadata Extraction Intervention Benchmark

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Using 300 weak-labeled examples derived from PubMed Central (PMC) natural documents, we compare a stripped-body retrieval baseline against a sequence of oracle-free interventions: preserved-text similarity, parsed-locator boosting, locator-window ranking, locator-local candidate expansion, and citation-neighborhood tie-breaking.

claims.claim: On the 161-example typed-locator subset, the best intervention (citation-neighborhood tie-breaking) yields answer exact accuracy of 0.6646, evidence top-1 of 0.5342, and MRR of 0.7318, compared to 0.3478, 0.2919, and 0.5241 for the stripped-body baseline.
